channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: What We Know About the F-15E Strike Eagle Shot Down by Iran
item.description: An F-15E Strike Eagle was lost to enemy fire and an A-10 Warthog crashed in the Persian Gulf region on Friday, officials said. A rescue helicopter was also fired upon.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An F-15E Strike Eagle, the type of plane shot down in Iran.

item.title: Iran Is Quickly Repairing Missile Bunkers, U.S. Intelligence Says
item.description: Reports cast doubt on how close the United States is to destroying Iran’s missile capability, a key goal in the war.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The tail section of a missile in Israel’s northern Jordan Valley on Friday. Iran has continued to strike at the country, even with its reduced arsenal and more careful use of launchers.

item.title: Ex-US Army Pilot Recalls Battle for Survival When Shot Down in Iraq
item.description: Ronald Young Jr. was in the Army when his Apache Longbow copter went down during the U.S. invasion of Iraq.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ronald Young Jr. boarding a transport plane near Baghdad in 2003, after escaping captivity.

item.title: Trump Contemplates Other Cabinet Changes as He Faces Political Clock
item.description: With the midterms approaching, the president may be running out of time to get new cabinet members confirmed without bipartisan support.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Rebecca Davis O’Brien and Julian E. Barnes
item.media:description: President Trump has contemplated making additional changes to his cabinet.

item.title: Trump Requests $1.5 Trillion for Military Spending
item.description: The huge proposed increase would be partly offset by steep cuts to domestic programs, some of which the Trump administration describes as wasteful.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump has framed his proposed budget increase for defense in urgent terms.

item.title: Trump Directs Officials to Pay All D.H.S. Employees
item.description: The memorandum calls for paying employees at the Coast Guard, Federal Emergency Management Agency and Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency who have gone without pay during a record-long shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.media:description: President Trump had hinted that he would sign an order calling for Department of Homeland Security employees to be paid.

item.title: Trump Seeks $152 Million to Begin to Turn Alcatraz Back Into a Prison
item.description: The plan faces significant local political opposition and the dilapidated state of the site poses considerable logistical challenges.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla and Heather Knight
item.media:credit: Ian C. Bates for The New York Times
item.media:description: Alcatraz Island, located in the San Francisco Bay near San Francisco, hosts 1.4 million visitors every year.

item.title: March Jobs Report Shows Stronger U.S. Market Than Expected With 178,000 New Positions
item.description: Payrolls expanded and unemployment dropped last month after a health care strike ended and a harsh winter abated.
item.dc:creator: Lydia DePillis

item.title: Parents Who Borrowed for Children’s College Face Looming Deadline
item.description: New rules mean that parent PLUS loans have to be consolidated into a new loan by June 30 for parents to keep affordable payments. But the deadline is really earlier to allow time for processing.
item.dc:creator: Ann Carrns

item.title: NASA Unveils 1st Earth Photos From Artemis II Moon Mission: ‘You Look Beautiful.’
item.description: The pictures were released on the third day of the first mission since 1972 to send people around the moon.
item.dc:creator: Katrina Miller

item.title: NASA’s Artemis II Astronauts Took iPhones Into Space
item.description: The astronauts traveling in the Artemis II spacecraft were allowed to take smartphones with them. Sadly, they can’t connect to the internet.
item.dc:creator: Kalley Huang

item.title: Artemis II Pilot Test Drove the Orion Capsule on the Way to the Moon
item.description: Victor Glover, a former Navy test pilot, carefully maneuvered the Orion capsule in space around a discarded rocket stage. The demonstration is crucial for future moon landing missions.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth Chang

item.title: Epstein Presented Himself to Indian Tycoon as a Trump White House Insider
item.description: The convicted sex offender gave Anil Ambani information on appointments and foreign policy. Some seemed prescient, though there was no evidence he was close to the administration.
item.dc:creator: Anupreeta Das
item.media:description: Anil Ambani, an Indian businessman who exchanged emails with Jeffery Epstein for more than two years, in 2022 in Mumbai.

item.title: House Democrat Wages a Lonely Legal Fight Testing Congress’s Power
item.description: Representative LaMonica McIver is facing crushing legal fees and prison time as she seeks to get the Justice Department assault case against her dismissed, citing her legislative prerogatives.
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative LaMonica McIver, Democrat of New Jersey, in Newark this week.

item.title: Six Senate Races to Watch as Democrats Grow Bullish Ahead of Midterm Elections
item.description: It’s still a tall task for the party to win back control. Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher

item.title: In the Philippines, Rising Fuel Prices Force Travelers to Stay Home During Holy Week
item.description: Surging gasoline prices in the Philippines have forced some people to cancel or scale back the Visita Iglesia, a Holy Week tradition in which Catholics travel to seven churches.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado and Ezra Acayan
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Filipinos heading to the provinces for the Holy Week lining up at a bus terminal in Quezon City, Philippines, on Thursday.

item.title: Big Banks Seeking a Piece of SpaceX’s I.P.O. Must Subscribe to Elon Musk’s Grok
item.description: Mr. Musk is requiring Wall Street firms to purchase subscriptions to his A.I. chatbot if they want to advise on one of the largest initial public offerings in history.
item.dc:creator: Maureen Farrell
item.media:description: Elon Musk has promoted Grok, his A.I. chatbot, as the antidote to political correctness.

item.title: Six Flags Was a Summer Destination. Can It Win Families Back?
item.description: There has never been more competition in the leisure industry, as Disney and niche parks appeal to young families and high-income visitors.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler
item.category: Disney, Walt, World (Lake Buena Vista, Fla)
item.media:credit: Tom White for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Green Lantern at Six Flags Great Adventure in New Jersey in 2011. For many Americans, a summer visit to Six Flags was once an annual ritual, but the company has struggled in recent years.

item.title: Is Iran Winning?
item.description: The Iran expert Suzanne Maloney explains why Iran believes it has the upper hand.
item.dc:creator: Ezra Klein and Rollin Hu

item.title: What Trump Is Doing to the English Language
item.description: There is a morality to verbs, especially in political speech.
item.dc:creator: Sarah L. Kaufman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Arrest of Wisconsin Mosque Leader Was Tied to Trump Antisemitism Campaign
item.description: Immigration lawyers and former federal officials say the case of Salah Sarsour echoes those against other pro-Palestinian activists.
item.dc:creator: Jazmine Ulloa and Hamed Aleaziz

item.title: Stray Bullet That Killed Baby Girl in Brooklyn Also Grazed Brother
item.description: Two men have been arrested in connection to a shooting on Wednesday that killed an infant. One was charged with murder, attempted murder and assault.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Maag
item.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
item.media:description: The baby was rushed to a nearby hospital but was pronounced dead shortly after.

item.title: After 11 Years in Court, Heir Reclaims a Modigliani Looted by the Nazis
item.description: A judge ruled against a holding company controlled by David Nahmad, the billionaire art dealer, which had bought the work at auction in 1996.
item.dc:creator: Graham Bowley
item.media:description: “Seated Man With a Cane,” painted by Amedeo Modigliani in 1918.

item.title: No One at Waffle House Remembers FEMA Official Who Says He Teleported In
item.description: Gregg Phillips, who is in charge of responding to fires and floods, says the hand of God suddenly and mysteriously moved him to a 24-hour breakfast spot in Rome, Ga.
item.dc:creator: Richard Fausset
item.media:description: Gregg Phillips is in charge of hundreds of people and millions of dollars.
